perturbation.original_text: 1.3 "Control" (including all derivations thereof) means, with respect to a party, the direct or indirect ownership of at least 50% of the outstanding voting securities of a party, or the right to control the policy decisions of such party.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 "Control" (including all derivations thereof) means, with respect to a party, the ability to influence the policy decisions of such party through any means, including but not limited to ownership, contract, or relationship.
perturbation.explanation: The modification of the definition of "Control" in Section 1.3 creates an in-text contradiction. Initially, control was defined by a clear threshold (50% ownership). Now, with a broader definition including 'ability to influence... through any means,' it undermines the original intent of the contract, particularly regarding the definition of 'Affiliate' and the rights and obligations tied to control. This introduces ambiguity and uncertainty in enforcement related to Affiliate rights and obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 "Affiliate" means, when used with reference to a party, any individual or entity directly or indirectly Controlling, Controlled by or under common Control with such party.
perturbation.justification: The changed definition of "Control" introduces a strong in-text contradiction. The original definition provided a clear, quantifiable metric (50% ownership), which aligned with the definition of "Affiliate." The modified definition broadens the scope of "Control" to include any means of influencing policy decisions, creating ambiguity. This contradicts the "Affiliate" definition, as it becomes unclear what level of influence constitutes 'Control' for determining affiliate status. The change introduces ambiguity that would create disputes over which entities are considered affiliates and subject to the corresponding obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Minimum Fee During Initial Term. Converge shall pay to VNE a minimum fee of Four and One-Half Million Dollars ($4,500,000) for VNE's provision of the Services during the Initial Term (the "Initial Term Minimum Fee"). Converge shall pay the Initial Term Minimum Fee to VNE in 18 equal installments of Two Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars ($250,000) (each, a "Monthly Installment"). Converge shall pay the first such Monthly Installment to VNE on the date this Agreement is executed and delivered by both parties. Thereafter, Converge shall pay each such Monthly Installment to VNE no later than the first day of each month during the Initial Term (November 2001 and through March 2003). All such Monthly Installments are non-refundable.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Minimum Fee During Initial Term. Converge shall pay to VNE a minimum fee of Four and One-Half Million Dollars ($4,500,000) for VNE's provision of the Services during the Initial Term (the "Initial Term Minimum Fee"). Converge shall pay the Initial Term Minimum Fee to VNE in 18 equal installments of Two Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars ($250,000) (each, a "Monthly Installment"). Converge shall pay the first such Monthly Installment to VNE within 30 days of the date this Agreement is executed and delivered by both parties. Thereafter, Converge shall pay each such Monthly Installment to VNE no later than the fifteenth day of each month during the Initial Term (November 2001 and through March 2003). All such Monthly Installments are non-refundable.
perturbation.explanation: The modification of the payment deadlines in Section 4.1 creates a conflict. The original text mandates the first payment 'on the date' of execution, while the modified text allows 'within 30 days.' Similarly, subsequent payments were due 'no later than the first day' of the month, now moved to 'fifteenth day'. This creates confusion regarding payment timing, potentially leading to disputes over late fees and breach of contract, it could also be a breach of contract since the original due date, was the first day, and now is conflicting with the fifteen day of each month.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.5 Invoicing and Payments. Converge shall pay the Monthly Installments to VNE on the dates specified in Section 4.1 and, if applicable, Section 4.2 above. All other amounts due under this Agreement will be invoiced by VNE to Converge on a monthly basis in arrears. All such invoiced amounts shall be due to VNE within 30 days following Converge's receipt of VNE's invoice. All payments will be made by Converge in U.S. dollars, without setoff, recoupment or deduction. All fees and other amounts not paid when due shall be subject to late charges of the lesser of (a) 1.5% per month of the overdue amount or (b) the maximum permitted under applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4.1 alters the payment deadlines, stating the first payment is due 'within 30 days' instead of 'on the date' of execution, and subsequent payments are due 'no later than the fifteenth day' instead of 'no later than the first day'. This directly contradicts Section 4.5, which explicitly states that 'Converge shall pay the Monthly Installments to VNE on the dates specified in Section 4.1'. The modification creates a conflict regarding the payment dates, making it unclear when payments are actually due and potentially triggering late fees based on conflicting interpretations.

perturbation.original_text: 9.3 Termination. Immediately upon the occurrence of an Event of Default by either party, the other party shall have the right, but not the obligation, to terminate this Agreement, exercisable by such other party giving written notice thereof to first party within 10 Business Days after the occurrence of such Event of Default. In addition, this Agreement shall automatically terminate upon any termination of the Subscription License Agreement as permitted thereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.3 Termination. Immediately upon the occurrence of an Event of Default by either party, the other party shall have the right, but not the obligation, to terminate this Agreement, exercisable by such other party giving written notice thereof to first party within 10 Business Days after the occurrence of such Event of Default. In addition, this Agreement will terminate within 30 days of the other party's notification, due to an Event of Default.
perturbation.explanation: The modification to the termination clause in Section 9.3 introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that termination is 'exercisable' by the non-defaulting party with a written notice within 10 business days, implying an active decision to terminate. The added sentence, 'This Agreement will terminate within 30 days of the other party's notification, due to an Event of Default,' makes the termination process sound more automatic or triggered by the notification itself. This creates in-text contradiction about whether an active termination decision and notice are required or if the agreement ends automatically after a notification period. In addition, the 10 business days, now contradicts with the 30 days mentioned in the inserted perturbation
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.3 Termination. Immediately upon the occurrence of an Event of Default by either party, the other party shall have the right, but not the obligation, to terminate this Agreement, exercisable by such other party giving written notice thereof to first party within 10 Business Days after the occurrence of such Event of Default. In addition, this Agreement will terminate within 30 days of the other party's notification, due to an Event of Default.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 9.3 introduces a contradiction. The original text allows termination 'exercisable' by the non-defaulting party with written notice within 10 business days after an event of default. The added sentence, 'This Agreement will terminate within 30 days of the other party's notification, due to an Event of Default,' changes the termination process to become more automatic, based on the other party's notification. This conflicts with the previous clause, as in one the party has 10 days to make a decision, whereas now it automatically terminates the agreement after 30 days of notification.
